metadata:
  rationale: "Capability detection is called from two places with different caching semantics: context-hook.ts caches (once per session), append-system.ts re-detects (every turn). Docs/contracts don't specify caching behavior. This is a real but low-severity gap — the system prompt shows current capability while the preamble may be stale."
  source: execution
  alternatives: ~
  tags: "capability-detection, caching, context-hook, append-system"
---
context-hook.ts uses once-per-session cached capability (module-level `_capability`) with no refresh mechanism, while append-system.ts re-detects fresh on every `before_agent_start`. This creates two potentially divergent capability sources in a single session — preamble (frozen) vs system prompt (current). The comment on context-hook.ts line 10 is misleading.
